subtopics.title: The Number System
common_misconceptions: Zero is not a whole number (it is)
The largest number exists (no largest number)
Place value and face value are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Place value: Each digit in a number has a value depending on its position. Example: In 3,456, 3 is thousands (3000), 4 is hundreds (400), 5 is tens (50), 6 is units (6). Reading large numbers: group in threes from right. Nigerian context: population numbers (200+ million), budget figures (Nigeria's budget in trillions of naira). Face value = digit itself. Place value = digit × position value.

subtopics.title: Basic Operations on Whole Numbers
common_misconceptions: Multiplication always makes numbers bigger (not with fractions/decimals)
BODMAS doesn't matter (it determines correct order)
subtopics.teaching_note: BODMAS: Brackets, Orders (powers), Division, Multiplication, Addition, Subtraction. Worked example: 3 + 4 × 2 = 3 + 8 = 11 (NOT 7 × 2 = 14). (3 + 4) × 2 = 7 × 2 = 14. Division with remainder: 17 ÷ 5 = 3 remainder 2. Check: 5 × 3 + 2 = 17. Word problems: identify key words - 'sum' means add, 'difference' means subtract, 'product' means multiply, 'shared equally' means divide.

subtopics.title: Types and Operations on Fractions
common_misconceptions: Fractions cannot be added directly (need common denominator)
Mixed numbers and improper fractions are different values (they're equal)
subtopics.teaching_note: Proper: numerator < denominator (3/4). Improper: numerator ≥ denominator (5/4). Mixed: whole number + fraction (1¾). Converting: Improper to mixed: 5/4 = 1¼. Mixed to improper: 1¾ = (4×1+3)/4 = 7/4. Equivalent fractions: multiply/divide numerator and denominator by same number. 1/2 = 2/4 = 3/6. Adding/subtracting: find common denominator (LCM of denominators). Multiplying: multiply numerators, multiply denominators. Dividing: multiply by reciprocal.

subtopics.title: LCM and HCF of Numbers
common_misconceptions: LCM is always larger than HCF (not necessarily)
Prime numbers are odd numbers (2 is prime and even)
1 is a prime number (NO, prime has exactly 2 factors)
subtopics.teaching_note: LCM (Least Common Multiple): smallest number that is a multiple of all given numbers. HCF/GCD (Highest Common Factor): largest number that divides all given numbers. Methods: 1) Listing multiples/factors. 2) Prime factorization. 3) Division method. Worked example: LCM of 12 and 18. 12 = 2² × 3, 18 = 2 × 3². LCM = 2² × 3² = 36. HCF = 2 × 3 = 6. Applications: LCM for finding common denominators, scheduling. HCF for simplifying fractions, dividing things equally.
